title: REGIONAL GUIDELINES FOR CODEX CONTACT POINTS AND NATIONAL CODEX COMMITTEES (ASIA)

sections.section: REGIONAL GUIDELINES FOR CODEX CONTACT POINTS AND NATIONAL CODEX COMMITTEES (ASIA)
sections.summary: Document title/heading only: Regional Guidelines for Codex Contact Points and National Codex Committees (Asia); not a substantive regulatory section.
Document identifier/header only: 'CAC GL/57-1999' (non‑substantive).
Heading 'Contents' only; not a substantive section.
Heading 'Introduction' only; not a substantive section.

Defines a Codex Contact Point as the national central coordination point for all Codex activities that receives and distributes Codex documents/publications, maintains a standards library, liaises with the National Codex Committee, government ministries, industry, consumers and other countries, and facilitates consultations and coordinated responses to Codex matters.

Explains the need to establish a National Codex Committee (NCC) to support the Contact Point and involve relevant ministries, industry, consumers and scientific organizations, with the NCC advising government on implications of food standardization and control to protect consumers and facilitate trade.

sections.section: Office of the National Codex Alimentarius Committee
sections.summary: Describes the Office of the NCC as the Contact Point and secretariat responsible for disseminating Codex documents, preparing national comments and acceptance via sub‑committees, coordinating delegations and meetings, conducting research where data are lacking, and implementing Codex standards/MRLs through existing laws.
Non‑substantive heading with an image placeholder; no procedural text for Codex work is provided.

sections.section: Procedure for Participating in Codex Meetings
sections.summary: Non‑substantive heading with an image placeholder; no text describing procedures for participating in Codex meetings is provided.

sections.section: Guideline for Distribution of Codex Documents
sections.summary: Provides distribution guidelines stating Codex documents and publications should be sent to authorized government bodies (e.g., Food and Drug Administration, ministries of health, commerce, agriculture, industry), libraries, experts, concerned departments and private sector entities, members of NCC sub‑committees, and on request.
Non‑substantive heading titled 'Other Activities' with no further content.

Introduces the purpose of food standards and Codex: to ensure product quality and food safety for consumer health and to facilitate fair international trade, and presents the Guidelines as reference examples for establishing Codex Contact Points and National Codex Committees while noting existing organizations may be used instead.

sections.section: Standardization  is  a  mechanism  to  ensure the  quality  products.    Food  standards  set  the requirements for products to ensure that they are safe and fit for consumption, thus providing protection of the health of consumers.
sections.summary: States that standardization ensures product quality and that food standards set requirements to make foods safe and fit for consumption, thereby protecting consumer health.

Explains that operation of Codex Contact Points varies by country and reiterates the Contact Point's role as the national coordination hub that distributes Codex documents, liaises with the NCC and stakeholders, serves as communications channel with the Codex Secretariat and other countries, and facilitates consultations and regional coordination.

Defines the Contact Point as the national coordination center that receives and maintains Codex documents and standards, works closely with or acts in lieu of a National Codex Committee to provide policy and technical advice, and serves as the communications link and facilitator of consultations and document distribution.

States the Contact Point should be a government function ideally located in the ministry responsible for food standards and control (or the most appropriate ministry based on resources and structure), be granted adequate authority and resources, and have its location and scope decided at ministerial level to ensure inter‑ministerial cooperation.

Requires notifying the Codex Secretariat of the Contact Point's name/address and publicizing its creation domestically, and providing necessary human, financial and physical resources including mail/communication facilities, storage, office equipment, clerical/translation support, record systems and adequate funding to perform its functions.

Reiterates the importance of establishing an NCC to supplement the Contact Point and involve ministries, agencies, industry and consumers in advising government on food standardization and control issues to protect consumers and support trade.

Specifies NCC purpose to advise government on food standards and control, recommends chairing by a senior official of the responsible ministry, sets membership to include relevant ministries, industry, trade, consumers and scientific bodies, mandates regular meetings (e.g., monthly) and establishment of balanced sub‑committees for technical subjects.

Lists NCC membership categories: senior ministry chair, relevant government ministries (health, agriculture, trade, etc.), scientific and research organizations, representatives of food industry sectors, trading/import–export sector, consumer groups, and individual expert members.

sections.section: 7. Individual  Members:  Frequently  there  will  be  individuals  who  possess  expert  knowledge  of particular aspects of food  processing or food control.  Often these are people who have worked in the  food  industry  or  in  research  organizations  in  the  past.  The  recruitment  of  such  persons with  special  qualifications  can  bring  expertise  to  the  National  Codex  Committee  which otherwise may not be available.
sections.summary: States that individual experts with specialized knowledge of food processing or control may be recruited to the NCC to provide technical expertise not otherwise available.

sections.section: ## OFFICE OF THE NATIONAL CODEX COMMITTEE
sections.summary: Affirms the Office of the NCC functions as the Contact Point and secretariat, responsible for disseminating Codex information (including translations and seminars), compiling national comments through sub‑committees, proposing acceptance actions to government, supporting meeting delegations, conducting collaborative research to obtain missing data, and facilitating enforcement of Codex standards/MRLs via existing laws.

Enumerates Office responsibilities: serve as NCC secretariat; act as the national contact point for the FAO/WHO programme; collect, procure and analyze data for international standards; assess practicability and trade impact of standards; conduct studies/research to resolve issues; encourage manufacturer quality and hygiene improvements; and disseminate standards and food law information to stakeholders.
Non‑substantive organization chart placeholder with no textual organizational detail provided.
Non‑substantive heading stating the NCC office organization may mirror the Codex Alimentarius Commission structure; contains an image placeholder.

sections.section: PROCEDURE FOR PARTICIPATING IN CODEX MEETINGS
sections.summary: Non‑substantive heading for procedures on participating in Codex meetings; section contains only an image placeholder and no procedural text.

sections.section: GUIDELINE FOR DISTRIBUTION OF CODEX DOCUMENTS
sections.summary: Guides distribution of Codex documents: send documents to authorized bodies (Food and Drug Administration; Ministries of Public Health, Commerce, Agriculture, Industry; interested experts), send publications to the standards body library, distribute subject‑specific documents to concerned departments, private sector and NCC subcommittee members, and provide documents by request.

sections.section: 1. All documents to authorized bodies such as:
sections.summary: Mandates that all Codex documents be sent to authorized bodies including the Food and Drug Administration, Ministries of Public Health, Commerce, Agriculture, Industry, and experts interested in Codex work.
Requires translation of Codex standards, codes and important publications into the local language and distribution to all interested persons, organizations and food manufacturers.

Directs the NCC to hold workshops and seminars to improve industry understanding of food standards and safety (quality control, personal hygiene, GMP, packaging and labelling) and to broadly communicate the Codex message.
Recommends using Codex/FAO/WHO publications to educate policymakers and the public and, where necessary, translating key materials into the local language to reach industry and consumers.
Encourages ongoing regional cooperation among Codex member countries for joint activities, sharing materials and experience, and promoting Codex principles to industry and governments.

Notes attendance at Codex meetings is often costly; advises NCCs to seek industry funding to send representatives and, if attendance is impossible, to ensure written views are submitted to the Codex Secretariat so members' positions are considered.

Calls for government support and funding of experiments or research projects, conducted with governmental and private local organizations, to generate data needed for draft standards or maximum limits.
Non‑substantive heading for other activities; no substantive text provided.
